com.mentor.chs.plugin.sbom

Interface IXSBOMEvaluationContext

  • All Superinterfaces:
    IXSBOMHarnessUtilities

    @NotToBeImplemented
public interface IXSBOMEvaluationContext
extends IXSBOMHarnessUtilities
    The SBOM evaluation context represents information about state of the SBOM used by processes that evaluate one sub-assembly of the SBOM at a time, exposing the notion of a current sub-assembly. For example, the SBOM engine(s) use this information to evaluate queries and expressions. An IXSBOMEvaluationContext object is available to evaluate filter and result expressions when 1) an SBOM is generated (in this case, the context is consumed by operation conditions and exclusions) 2) when cost results are generated for an SBOM (in this case, the context is consumed by operation task instances and formulas)

    This Interface also provides utilities that allow interaction with an SBOM's processed harness design.

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Abstract Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      java.lang.String getCurrentFormulaElementFilterName()
      Returns the name of the filter expression associated with the current formula element in the costing process.
      java.lang.String getCurrentFormulaName()
      Returns the name of the current formula in the costing process.
      IXSubAssembly getCurrentSubAssembly()
      The current sub-assembly being processed by the SBOM engine.
      java.lang.String getCurrentTaskName()
      Returns the name of the current task in the costing process.
      IXSBOMContext getSBOMContext()
      SBOMContext passed from the SBOM engine - allows interrogation of design objects / sub-assemblies.
      IXSubAssembly getSignificantParent(IXSubAssembly subAssembly)
      For a given child sub-assembly, return the significant parent in this context, i.e.

      • Methods inherited from interface com.mentor.chs.plugin.sbom.IXSBOMHarnessUtilities

        getLongestPathInHarness, getLongestWireLengthInHarness

    • Method Detail

      • getSBOMContext

        IXSBOMContext getSBOMContext()
        SBOMContext passed from the SBOM engine - allows interrogation of design objects / sub-assemblies.
        Returns:
        the current SBOM engine context

      • getCurrentSubAssembly

        IXSubAssembly getCurrentSubAssembly()
        The current sub-assembly being processed by the SBOM engine. For example, when cost results are generated, the sub-assembly which is being costed. For example, this sub-assembly can be used to determine if a related object is also present in the same sub-assembly.
        Returns:
        the current sub assembly

      • getSignificantParent

        IXSubAssembly getSignificantParent(IXSubAssembly subAssembly)
        For a given child sub-assembly, return the significant parent in this context, i.e. given the current sub-assembly. This is used to determine whether two objects are being assembled in the current sub-assembly. For example, to determine if a wire end is being inserted into its connector cavity. In this case, the wire end and the connector would have different significant parent sub-assemblies within the current sub-assembly, whereas if the significant parent sub-assemblies are the same, the connection has already occurred in a child sub-assembly.
        Parameters:
        subAssembly - the sub-assembly being evaluated.
        Returns:
        the significant parent sub-assembly.
        See Also:
        getCurrentSubAssembly()

      • getCurrentTaskName

        java.lang.String getCurrentTaskName()
        Returns the name of the current task in the costing process.
        Returns:
        The name of the current task

      • getCurrentFormulaName

        java.lang.String getCurrentFormulaName()
        Returns the name of the current formula in the costing process.
        Returns:
        The name of the current formula

      • getCurrentFormulaElementFilterName

        java.lang.String getCurrentFormulaElementFilterName()
        Returns the name of the filter expression associated with the current formula element in the costing process. If the current formula element has no associated filter expression ("any"), then null is returned.
        Returns:
        The filter expression name
